description: Tool-selection gold set (SPEC tool-surface measurement). Each task names the tool(s) that SHOULD be selected. `profile` is where the correct tool lives in surface.json — tasks whose profile != core stress the recall risk of hard gating.

cases.prompt: Shade this rectangle (x8 y10 w12 h6) with an ordered Bayer dither between skin-mid and skin-dark at 40%.
Re-map this rough grey blob so every colour snaps onto my green ramp dark->light.
These pixels drifted off-palette — snap them back to my locked palette using perceptual (CIELAB) colour distance.
Extract a machine-checkable style profile (native grid, ramps with lint, light direction, heads-tall) from the active sprite.
Rotate the selected cel about 30 degrees onto a new layer without introducing any new colours.
I have a 4-frame walk sprite-sheet PNG (1 row). Bring it in as an Aseprite animation on a shared 12-colour palette.
Save one image that shows every animation frame side by side so I can review the walk-cycle timing.
Add a forward-loop animation tag named 'walk' spanning frames 1 to 4.
Turn my painted level mockup layer into a deduplicated tileset plus a tilemap that reconstructs it.
I drew the 4 corner quarters (fill/outer/edge/inner). Generate the full 47-tile blob autotile sheet from them.
Rename the layer currently called 'Layer 1' to 'Body'.
Export the sprite as a packed spritesheet PNG plus a JSON data file that includes the frame tags.
Draw this batch of 20 specific coloured pixels onto the Body layer.
Give me a vision-legible upscaled preview of the sprite with a labelled coordinate gutter so I can read off pixel positions.
Convert this reference photo into 48x48 palette-locked pixel art on a Reference layer so I can trace over it.
